sense.definition: to let something happen or exist

examples.x: The large venue can admit of a substantial audience for the concert.
This plan admits of various approaches to problem-solving.
The design of the software should admit of future updates and improvements.
The contract should admit of renegotiation if necessary.

sense.definition: using the previous knowledge, reasoning, or general facts to decide the likely result of something
very probably; most possibly
to be likely for someone to succeed in doing something
used to show the possibility of something happening or being the case
very likely to happen or be the case

examples.x: With his extensive experience, he could easily land the job at any top company.
She could easily win the competition with her talent and dedication.
The weather could easily change, so it's best to bring an umbrella just in case.
The new policy could easily improve the company's overall efficiency.
He could easily pass the exam if he continues to study diligently.

sense.definition: anticipated or predicted to happen based on previous knowledge or assumptions

examples.x: The students were prepared for the expected test questions after studying the material thoroughly.
The expected arrival time of the train is 3:00 PM, but there may be delays due to track maintenance.
The doctor explained the expected side effects of the medication to the patient before prescribing it.

sense.definition: ‌used to express the probability of something
to add information or details to something in order to make it seem more credible or probable
used to point out the probability of something
having a possibility of happening or being the case

examples.x: The doctor believes it's likely that the patient will make a full recovery with proper treatment.
The dark clouds indicate a likely chance of rain later in the day.
Based on recent trends, it's likely that the company will see an increase in profits next quarter.
With her impressive qualifications and experience, she's a likely candidate for the job.
The recent increase in sales makes it a likely scenario that the company will expand its operations.

sense.definition: to be likely to occur or to appear to be the case

examples.x: It looks as though she's going to be late for the appointment.

sense.definition: used to express a possibility

examples.x: It might rain later this evening.
She might attend the party if she finishes her work on time.
We might go to the beach if the weather is nice.
He might be able to help you with your project.
They might offer discounts during the holiday season.

sense.definition: used to say that something is likely to happen or is true

examples.x: The quality of her work was excellent, there's no doubt about it.
He will be late for the meeting, no doubt due to the traffic.
The team's victory was achieved through hard work and determination, no doubt.
The company's success is no doubt attributed to its innovative strategies.
She will win the competition, no doubt about her skills.

sense.definition: used to emphasize the fact that someone's expectations or wishes are very unlikely to be fulfilled

examples.x: You’ll be lucky!
Sure, procrastinate until the night before the exam, and you will be so lucky to even pass.
Leave your car unlocked again, and you will be so lucky if it's still there in the morning.
Forget sunscreen at the beach, and you will be so lucky if you don't turn into a lobster.
Miss all your classes, and you will be lucky if you magically ace all your exams.

sense.definition: without any specific plans or assurance of success
used to talk about what one expects or likes to happen

examples.x: He ought to arrive soon; he said he would be here by noon.
The project ought to be completed by the end of the month, according to the schedule.
They ought to win the game, considering their strong performance in recent matches.
We ought to hear back from them by tomorrow about the job offer.
The repair ought to fix the issue with the leaking faucet.

sense.definition: one's thoughts or expectations regarding what will happen in the future

examples.x: Despite facing challenges, she maintained a positive outlook on her career, believing that hard work would lead to success.
The team's strong performance in recent matches has improved the coach's outlook for the upcoming championship.
Despite the economic downturn, his outlook on the company's future remained optimistic.
Her positive outlook on life helped her overcome many personal challenges.
The weather forecast gave a gloomy outlook for the weekend, with heavy rain expected.

sense.definition: appearing contradictory or conflicting but potentially true

examples.x: The concept of time is paradoxical; it's both infinite and finite depending on how it's perceived.
The artist's work often explores paradoxical themes, such as the coexistence of beauty and decay.
The paradoxical nature of quantum mechanics challenges traditional understandings of reality.
The paradoxical idea of "less is more" suggests that simplicity can often be more impactful than complexity.
It's paradoxical that the more choices we have, the harder it becomes to make a decision.

sense.definition: used to express possibility or likelihood of something

examples.x: He is late, perhaps he missed the bus.
Perhaps the weather will improve by the time we start our outdoor event.
She will perhaps consider the job offer, but she's exploring other options.
She hasn't decided yet, but perhaps she'll join us later.
Perhaps there is a better solution we haven't considered yet.

sense.definition: possibility refers to the state or condition of being able to happen or exist, or a potential likelihood of something happening or being true
able to exist, happen, or be done

examples.x: Even when it seems unlikely, making new friends in a new city is possible.
I will support you in every possible way.
It is possible to learn a new language at any age.
The team made every possible effort to win the game.
To achieve the best possible result, we need to work together.

sense.definition: used to say that the something is believed to be true based on available information or evidence

examples.x: She left the office early, presumably to attend a family event.
The concert tickets sold out quickly, presumably due to the band's recent popularity.
The CEO's sudden resignation is presumably linked to the company's financial challenges.
The car was parked in a no-parking zone, presumably because there were no available spaces nearby.
The project deadline was extended, presumably to allow more time for thorough research and development.

sense.definition: a belief that something is true without any proof
based on the likelihood of an event or outcome occurring

examples.x: The weather forecast is a probabilistic model that predicts the chance of rain.
The probabilistic nature of quantum mechanics suggests that particles can exist in multiple states simultaneously.
Statistical analysis often involves examining probabilistic relationships between variables.
Probabilistic algorithms are used in machine learning to make predictions based on uncertain data.
In gambling, players assess the probabilistic outcomes before placing their bets.
Probabilistic reasoning helps in making decisions under uncertainty.

sense.definition: the likelihood or chance of an event occurring or being true

examples.x: The probability of winning the lottery is extremely low.
There is a high probability of rain tomorrow based on the weather forecast.
The researchers calculated the probability of success for their experiment.
He assessed the probability of passing the exam based on his study habits.
Understanding probability is essential in making informed decisions in gambling and finance.

sense.definition: having a high possibility of happening or being true based on available evidence or circumstances

examples.x: With clear skies and no signs of storm, it seems probable that the outdoor event will proceed as planned.
The economist predicts that there is a probable increase in inflation rates next quarter.
The detective considered the suspect's alibi to be probable, as it was supported by multiple witnesses.
The survey results suggest that there is a probable decline in consumer confidence in the current economic climate.
The archaeologist believes it's probable that the ancient ruins discovered belong to a previously unknown civilization.

sense.definition: used to show likelihood or possibility without absolute certainty

examples.x: She will probably arrive at the party after 8 PM.
I probably left my keys on the kitchen counter.
It will probably rain later in the evening, so bring an umbrella.
The package will probably be delivered by the end of the week.
He is probably going to join us for dinner tonight.

sense.definition: ‌a thing that is extremely likely to be true or happen

examples.x: Investing in government bonds is often seen as a safe bet for long-term financial stability.
Choosing the well-established brand is usually a safe bet when it comes to quality.
In a competitive job market, having a strong educational background is a safe bet for career advancement.

sense.definition: prepared or likely prepared for something
to have a likelihood of success or achieving a desired outcome

examples.x: She was so nervous during the interview that she didn't think she stood a chance of getting the job.
Without a good plan, our project doesn't stand a chance of succeeding.
Even in the face of adversity, if you believe in yourself, you stand a chance of overcoming challenges.
With the right investments, our business can stand a chance of becoming successful.
Although the odds are against us, we still stand a chance of turning things around.

sense.definition: (particularly of something bad) assumed to have happened or be the case without having any proof

examples.x: The authorities evacuated the building due to a suspected gas leak, even though they hadn't detected any fumes.
The manager fired the employee over a suspected breach of company policy, despite lacking concrete evidence.
The journalist wrote an article about the suspected corruption within the government, which stirred significant public interest.
The medical team quarantined the patient due to a suspected case of a highly contagious disease, pending test results.
The committee launched an investigation into the suspected embezzlement of funds after noticing irregularities in the accounts.

sense.definition: extremely likely

examples.x: No one knows for sure how they're going to deal with these sales issues, but ten to one it's the employees who have to suffer the cost.
Considering the dark clouds and thunder, it's ten to one it's going to rain soon.
Based on her track record, it's ten to one that she'll win the competition again.
Given the traffic at this hour, it's ten to one we'll be late for the meeting.
The team's performance has been outstanding, so it's ten to one they'll secure the championship.

sense.definition: used to indicate that there is a high chance for something to happen or be true
used to express what one deems true or probable
the likelihood or probability of something actually taking place

examples.x: The odds of winning the grand prize in the raffle are one in a thousand.
She calculated the odds of getting a heads in a single coin toss as 50%.
The doctor explained the odds of recovery, giving the patient hope despite the seriousness of the condition.
Investors weighed the odds of success before deciding to fund the startup.
